results.title: Does anyone have a good alternative to Privacy. ...
results.content: | |  |  | lobstersammich on Jan 22, 2023  | parent | context | favorite | on: Detect breaches with Canary credit cards   Does anyone have a good alternative to Privacy.com where your virtual credit card transaction data isn't sold to Wall Street? If you're unfamiliar with what a "virtual [credit] card" is here's the page from Privacy.com's website:  I use the Privacy app on my mobile phone to create virtual cards (primarily for work subscriptions). Pro-tip: since each Privacy card can have its own name put a tag such as `[WORK\_RECURRING]` into the card name and then you can search your email inbox for `[WORK\_RECURRING]`, quickly and easily finding all of the transactions / charges that you may want to submit to your workplace for reimbursement. Privacy is owned / created by Lithic, but [...] reimbursement. Privacy is owned / created by Lithic, but if you look at Lithic's investors you'll see that the plurality of the company's investors are in the private equity or VC space: Bessemer Ventures, Tusk Partner Ventures, Index Ventures, etc. You can see the Privacy.com / Privacy mobile app's funders here:   Thus, I have no doubt that my transactions on cleverly-named Privacy app are being gifted or sold to Wall Street so that hedge funds can squeeze out a few addition drops of 'signal' from consumer purchase pattern data that would otherwise remain dark. (I'd imagine that many folks use the Privacy app to buy things that they'd rather not have show up on their regular credit card bills: 'adult websites', marijuana or tobacco products, etc.  So, two questions:  (1) Does anyone have [...] products, etc.  So, two questions:  (1) Does anyone have a privacy-respecting alternative to Privacy.com's virtual credit cards?  (2) Does anyone know of a recent blog post where these virtual credit card services are compared / contrasted by  - the services that they offer, - the cost: free, paid, etc., - the terms of service: how your data is re-sold / who your data is transmitted to |     |  |  |  --- | | |  |  | asciimike on Jan 22, 2023  | next (javascript:void(0))   > (1) Does anyone have a privacy-respecting alternative to Privacy.com's virtual credit cards? Capital One offers virtual cards through Eno () that are merchant locked. They make it somewhat cumbersome to use, but I've really enjoyed using them.  It doesn't block wall street knowing about what you're buying, but at least

results.title: 8+ Best Privacy.com Alternatives for Virtual Cards [2024]
results.content:
‍

## Privacy.com Alternatives

Here are the best alternatives to Privacy.com:

‍

### 1. Cloaked

Cloaked is a privacy-first platform designed to provide users with complete online invisibility and optimal security. It is also set to roll out “Cloaked Pay,” a feature that gives users an anonymous virtual card to use for shopping online safely so they never have to input their real credit card details on any e-commerce website again. [...] ‍

## Use the Best Privacy.com Alternative

There are many alternatives to Privacy.com, but not all provide complete privacy. A good privacy app not only protects its users from data breaches and brushing scams but also secures their personal data.

In addition to offering virtual cards, Cloaked has extensive features for individual online security. It offers client-based encryption that shields its users' personal information and puts them in control of their data so that even Cloaked can not access it.

Cloaked not only stores and protects your passwords but also imports them from other password managers, supports multiple identity management, and uses multi-factor authentication for maximum security. [...] ‍

### What is the best anonymous virtual card?

The best anonymous virtual card is offered by Cloaked via Cloaked Pay.  The Cloaked Pay virtual card is an anonymous card with unique card details. Users can easily create one on the Cloaked app or desktop dashboard.

With Cloaked’s anonymous virtual card, users can create a card to make purchases online and delete the card once they have no use for it.

‍

### What is the best Privacy.com alternative?

The best Privacy.com alternative is Cloaked. Use Cloaked if you want a platform that offers complete privacy and functionality. Cloaked provides all the features and functionalities needed to transact online while remaining completely anonymous.

‍

## Use the Best Privacy.com Alternative

results.title: 9 Best Privacy.com Alternatives for Virtual Cards in 2026
results.content:
## 9 best Privacy.com alternatives

These Privacy.com alternatives offer virtual card services with varying features for personal and business use.

### 1. Ramp

Ramp offers unlimited virtual cards with built-in expense management, making it a strong Privacy.com alternative for businesses. You can create cards instantly, set custom spend limits, and automate receipt matching across your entire team.

#### Key features [...] ## Why look for a Privacy.com alternative

Privacy.com works well for individual consumers, but several limitations push users toward alternatives—especially those who need business features, international access, or higher card volumes.

### US-only availability

Privacy.com only works for U.S. residents with a U.S. checking or savings account. If you live abroad, travel frequently, or run a business with international team members, you'll need a Privacy.com alternative that supports global access.

### Strict monthly card limits

Even the Premium plan caps you at 60 cards per month. If you manage dozens of subscriptions or pay multiple vendors, you'll hit those limits fast. That's why many users switch to platforms with higher or unlimited card creation. [...] ### International payments and currency support

If you pay vendors or shop internationally, multi-currency support reduces fees and friction. Privacy.com processes everything in USD and adds foreign transaction fees on the free tier.

Alternatives like Wise, Revolut, and Payoneer handle multiple currencies natively and offer better exchange rates—often at or near the mid-market rate.

## Ramp's business credit card: The best Privacy.com alternative

Ramp's Business Credit Card consistently ranks as the best Privacy.com competitor because it offers more than just unlimited virtual cards. With expense management automation, accounts payable, procurement, and corporate travel booking built in, it's a complete finance operations platform for modern business.

Been using halocard as an alternative, works internationally and no US residency needed, figured worth adding since this thread still comes up.

- Hey there, Privacy here! Please reach out to support@privacy.com and one of our agents will be happy to help you with this.
- It happened to me but they enable the account after reporting to them. They pause the account to make sure no one took over your cards or account
- The same happened to me with Privacy.com, and their support was useless. I switched to IronVest (formerly Blur ) and have never looked back.
- Ironvest [...] ## Content
I am a long time user of privacy.com . I have a paid account. Most of my monthly bills are setup to use privacy.com virtual cards. Today I went to make a purchase from a vendor I have used in the past without any issue. I even used the same privacy.com card as my previous purchases. Except this time, I received an automated email saying that they had fraud reported from that vendor and my account was now suspended. So here I am, looking for a service similar to privacy.com . Preferably one with a better policy in place when it comes to closing down accounts without warning. I understand I could appeal to them and hope they reopen my account. But I should also be proactive in the event that they won't. I don't want my power or phone bill going in late because of this. [...] # Privacy.com alternatives for individuals?

results.title: Best Search Engines for your Privacy - EthicalAds
results.content:
## Recommendations

Unless you have specific concerns, you can't go too far wrong with any of these privacy-minded alternatives. As an every day search engine, DuckDuckGo is popular with the EthicalAds and Read the Docs teams while respecting your privacy. For a search engine completely separate from the tech titans, try Mojeek or Brave Search and for a more developer focused search experience, both You.com and DuckDuckGo are great.

Do you disagree with our recommendations or you have something to add? Get in touch. We always love to hear from you.

### Become an advertiser

Start your campaign today!

### Become a publisher

Start earning money from your site [...] Fortunately, there's a lot of great alternatives and the right one depends on exactly what you're looking for in a search engine.

### DuckDuckGo

DuckDuckGo - a privacy search engine

DuckDuckGo search engine

DuckDuckGo has become synonymous, at least among techies, as the private alternative to Google. The search engine and the company has built their brand on privacy. In our teardown, DuckDuckGo scored top marks for a lack of cookies and persistent storage and no third-party requests. It's just a few clicks away to make it your browser's default search engine, and when the results aren't quite what you're looking for, you can prefix your query with `!g` to search Google. [...] Like DuckDuckGo, Brave has desktop and mobile apps so you can search privately everywhere. They claim not to store searches, clicks, or any other data and allow truly anonymous searches. Brave specifically mentions that they do not filter or downrank any search results. For a good alternative from a team focused on privacy, you can't go wrong with Brave Search.

### Other alternatives

SwissCows search engine

SwissCows search engine homepage

There are even more privacy-focused search engines that we did not detail in our privacy teardown. You might consider these search engines if you have particular concerns about data jurisdiction. From a quality perspective, most of these search engines rely on search indexes from other search engines.
